title: #claude Chat vs. Cowork: Biggest 3 differences
summary: Claude Cowork is positioned as an advanced variant of Claude Chat with three key improvements: direct file access from the user's computer without upload limits or file size caps, a larger context window that preserves conversation details longer, and integrated output generation that produces ready-to-use files directly in folders rather than chat-based responses.
writeup: A concise breakdown of three practical differences between Claude Chat and Claude Code, aimed at daily Chat users considering the step up. The core argument is that Claude Code removes the friction of file size limits, context compaction, and manual output handling by working directly on the local filesystem. A receipt-to-Excel demo illustrates the output delivery difference concretely.
key_points: Claude Code reads files directly from the local computer, removing Chat's 20-file-per-conversation and 30 MB-per-file ceilings that exist because Chat uploads files to the cloud.
Claude Code operates with a larger context window, so it begins compacting the conversation and discarding details later than Chat does, keeping more project context in view for longer.
Claude Code delivers ready-to-use output files directly into a local folder rather than returning a response the user must still act on manually.
In a live example, Claude Code processed 100-plus receipts and produced a completed Excel expense report with rows automatically flagged for review, requiring no manual copy-paste step.
